_provenance.source: AerialWaste MCML m2 split (5 classes), AW v2.0 dataset
_provenance.note: Cues hand-written for aerial-view identification. Categories are broader than the DroneWaste paper-10 set: 'Bulky items' subsumes furniture/appliances; 'Containers' includes anything drum/tank/IBC-like; 'Unknown material' is the explicit catch-all when the material type cannot be identified from above.

Rubble.aerial_cue: irregular piles of broken concrete, bricks, plaster, stones or masonry — grey/beige, dusty, irregular heaps

Bulky items.ewc_description: Bulky household waste (furniture, appliances, mattresses, white goods).
Bulky items.aerial_cue: large household objects — mattresses, sofas, fridges, washing machines, cabinets — recognisable furniture/appliance silhouettes too big for normal bins

Plastic.ewc_description: Plastic waste in all forms (sheets, films, packaging, bulk objects).
Plastic.aerial_cue: plastic sheets, films, bags, tarps, bulk plastic objects — bright colours (white, blue, green), smooth/glossy surfaces, often wind-blown or in piles

Containers.ewc_description: Containers: drums, IBC totes, oil tanks, large bins, packaging.
Containers.aerial_cue: regular-shape containers — cylindrical drums (~60 cm), rectangular IBC totes (~1 m³), tanks, big bins; rusty-brown, painted bright colours, often clustered or in rows

Unknown material.ewc_description: Waste whose material type cannot be unambiguously identified.
Unknown material.aerial_cue: indistinguishable refuse heap — material type unclear from above, neither obviously rubble nor plastic nor metal nor bulky
